Function to send a ping if the minimum time since the last ping has elapsed.

# Purpose
The code is a C header file that declares a function [`ping_send_if_due`](<#ping_send_if_due>). This function is responsible for sending a ping if the conditions are met, specifically when a minimum time interval of one hour has passed since the last ping. The function is intended to be called when other data is being sent to a phone, to reduce the frequency of waking the phone. The use of `#pragma once` ensures that the header file is included only once in a single compilation, preventing duplicate definitions.
# Function Declarations (Public API)

---
### ping\_send\_if\_due<!-- {{#callable_declaration:ping_send_if_due}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../src/fw/services/common/ping.h#L23>)

Send a ping if the minimum time period has elapsed since the last ping.
- **Description**: Use this function to send a ping when the minimum time period since the last ping has elapsed. It is designed to be called when other data is being sent to the phone to reduce the frequency of phone wake-ups. The function will not send a ping if the minimum time period has not passed or if a ping is already scheduled.
- **Inputs**: None
- **Output**: None
- **See Also**: [`ping_send_if_due`](<ping.c.md#ping_send_if_due>)  (Implementation)
